title: Diagnosing Closed Defaults
subtitle: Minimal Tests for Editable Governance after SDeltaPhi-29
core_thesis: Editability claimed is not editability real; editable governance must be diagnosed operationally through exercised revision, timely rollback, material exception re-entry, and materially distributed authority.
core_formula: D* editable-real iff Rv and Rb and Ex and Ad
closure_drift: not Rv or not Rb or not Ex or not Ad implies D* drifts toward closure regime
one_sentence: SDeltaPhi-30 provides minimal tests for distinguishing formal openness from operational re-openability in default governance.
negative_constraints: Do not treat declared openness as operational editability.
Do not treat feedback collection as exception re-entry.
Do not treat consultation as distributed authority.
Do not treat an appeal button as rollback reality.
Do not infer closure solely from a default's existence.
